design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore overworld and dungeons → Engage in turn-based ATB battles → Gain experience and ability points → Unlock new jobs → Master job abilities to customize party → Progress story
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Strategic job/ability selection versus flexible character customization; managing party composition against escalating enemy challenges
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a master tactician who freely experiments with job combinations and character builds to overcome diverse combat scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavily emphasizes player agency through the Job System—player freedom to master all 22 jobs and create bespoke character builds is the defining philosophy, rejecting rigid character roles

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through job selection and ability customization; community hacks document 'EasyType' and 'Expert' variants, indicating base game is tuned for intermediate players seeking strategic depth

progression.upgrade_system: Job System: characters earn Ability Points alongside experience points; mastering jobs unlocks permanent abilities usable by other jobs. Crystal shards gate access to new job classes
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated crystal shards unlock access to new jobs (22 base jobs, 26 in GBA version); side quests provide optional content
replayability_hooks: Job mastery encourages experimentation with different job combinations across playthroughs
Community ROM hacks document 'FF5 Ancient Cave' roguelike mode and 'FF5 Plus' with altered mechanics
Multiple difficulty variants available (EasyType, Expert) suggest intended replay with different challenge assumptions

world_building_techniques: Environmental exploration via traversable overworld connecting towns, dungeons, and points of interest
NPC dialogue in towns provides story context and optional side quest hooks
Story-critical encounters reveal plot through character interaction (e.g., discovering danger to four Crystals sealing Exdeath)
Multiple traversal methods (Chocobo, ship, airship) unlock world areas progressively, gating narrative beats

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as skill gates testing party composition and job ability mastery; recurring antagonist Gilgamesh appears across story acts
notable_bosses: Exdeath (primary antagonist, sealed by four Crystals, serves as overarching threat)
Gilgamesh (recurring encounter, documented in PlayStation promotional media)

faction_indicators: Four Crystals (elemental control) serve as central world conflict markers—crystal corruption/exploitation indicates Exdeath's growing influence
danger_signals: Fallen meteor event triggers story; crystal instability signals escalating threat
Exdeath's resurging power manifests through corrupted areas and enemy escalation
environmental_storytelling: Overworld topology reveals world state progression (new traversal methods unlock new regions)
Town NPC populations and dialogue reflect story progression and world threats
Crystal locations and shrine design reinforce elemental theming
